channel.title: Azure News Feed
channel.description: Aggregated daily news from Azure blogs

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Enhancements to maintenance notifications in Azure Service Health
item.description: We’re introducing regional level consolidated maintenance notifications for Azure Database for PostgreSQL to simplify how you stay informed about upcoming maintenance. Instead of one notification per server, now you receive a single notification per regio
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: PgBouncer 1.25.1 support in Azure Database for PostgreSQL – Flexible Server
item.description: PgBouncer 1.25.1 is now generally available in Azure Database for PostgreSQL. As a built-in connection pooling feature, PgBouncer helps you scale to thousands of connections with low overhead by efficiently managing idle and short-lived connections.With t
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Observability in AKS Namespace and Workload Views
item.description: AKS now surfaces observability data powered by Azure Monitor managed service for Prometheus directly within Namespace and Workload views, making it easier to:Monitor cluster and workload healthTroubleshoot pending or failed podsAnalyze resource utilizatio
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Azure Files assessments are now available using Azure Migrate
item.description: Azure Migrate now supports assessments for Azure Files, allowing customers and partners to efficiently plan migrations of on-premises SMB and NFS file shares (Windows and Linux) to Azure Files. This feature enables discovery of file shares, visibility int
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Visual Studio Code 1.115
item.description: Learn what's new in Visual Studio Code 1.115 Read the full article
item.dc:creator: Visual Studio Code Team

item.title: Code Security risk assessment available for organizations
item.description: Organization admins and security managers can now run a free Code Security risk assessment to review security vulnerabilities across their organization. The assessment summarizes vulnerabilities by severity, rule type, and… The post Code Security risk assessment available for organizations appeared...
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: GitHub Universe is back: We want you to take the stage
item.description: Get inspired by five of the most memorable, magical, and quirky Universe sessions to date. The post GitHub Universe is back: We want you to take the stage appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Cohen

item.title: New PGP signing key for GitHub CLI Linux packages
item.description: We’ve published an updated PGP keyring for GitHub CLI’s Linux package repositories. The keyring now includes both the current signing key and a new replacement key. If you’ve already rerun… The post New PGP signing key for GitHub CLI Linux packages appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: Introducing SQL MCP Server
item.description: SQL MCP Server gives enterprises a secure, feature-rich way to enable agents to access data. This is accomplished without exposing the schema, risking consistency, or relying on fragile natural language parsing. SQL MCP Server is a feature of Data API builder, so deployments have a proven entity...
item.dc:creator: Jerry Nixon

item.title: Give your Foundry Agent Custom Tools with MCP Servers on Azure Functions
item.description: Learn how to connect your MCP server hosted on Azure Functions to Microsoft Foundry agents. This post covers authentication options and setup steps to give your AI agent access to custom MCP tools. The post Give your Foundry Agent Custom Tools with MCP Servers on Azure Functions appeared first on...
item.dc:creator: Lily Ma

item.title: Azure Container Storage v2.1.0: Now GA with Elastic SAN
item.description: Explore what’s new in Azure Container Storage v2.1.0 with Elastic SAN integration, modular on-demand installation, and enhanced node selector support.
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: MCP as Easy as 1-2-3: Introducing the Fluent API for MCP Apps
item.description: Build MCP tools with rich UI experiences using the new Fluent API for MCP Apps in the .NET isolated worker. Configure views, permissions, and security policies with just a few lines of code. The post MCP as Easy as 1-2-3: Introducing the Fluent API for MCP Apps appeared first on Azure SDK Blog.
item.dc:creator: Lilian Kasem (she/her)

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Red Hat OpenShift adds NVIDIA H100 and H200 GPU support
item.description: Azure Red Hat OpenShift (ARO) now supports NVIDIA H100 and H200 GPU-based Azure Virtual Machine SKUs, enabling customers to run large-scale AI, machine learning, and high-performance computing (HPC) workloads on a fully managed OpenShift service. This upd
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Your Migration’s Source of Truth: The Modernization Assessment
item.description: Introducing GitHub Copilot Modernization and diving into the assessment report as part of cloud modernization process The post Your Migration’s Source of Truth: The Modernization Assessment appeared first on .NET Blog.
item.dc:creator: Jeffrey Fritz

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Disable HTTP proxy in AKS
item.description: Organizations that use HTTP proxies to control outbound traffic often need to change or remove those settings as network requirements evolve, which can be disruptive in running clusters. Azure Kubernetes Service allows administrators to disable HTTP proxy
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Functions now supports MCP resource triggers
item.description: Developers building MCP servers on Azure Functions can expose tools but not resources. Now, Azure Functions supports MCP resource triggers, enabling developers to expose resources directly from an Azure Functions–hosted Model Context Protocol (MCP) server
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Pod CIDR expansion in AKS
item.description: When clusters exhaust available pod IP addresses, teams have historically been forced to rebuild environments to restore capacity, creating operational disruption. Pod CIDR expansion allows overlay‑based AKS clusters to increase their pod IP range in plac
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Agentic development aspirations: build, run, observe — without more Markdown
item.description: AI agents can write code but struggle to run and test it. Aspire gives your agent a codified app model to build, run, and observe your whole stack. The post Agentic development aspirations: build, run, observe — without more Markdown appeared first on Aspire Blog.
item.dc:creator: Maddy Montaquila

item.title: ASP.NET Core 2.3 end of support announcement
item.description: ASP.NET Core 2.3 will reach end of support on April 7, 2027. Learn what this means for your applications and how to upgrade to a modern supported version of .NET. The post ASP.NET Core 2.3 end of support announcement appeared first on .NET Blog.
item.dc:creator: Daniel Roth

item.title: Scalable AI with Azure Cosmos DB: Tredence Intelligent Document Processing (IDP) | March 2026
item.description: Azure Cosmos DB enables scalable AI-driven document processing, addressing one of the biggest barriers to operational scale in today’s enterprise AI landscape. Organizations continue to manage massive volumes of structured and unstructured content—contracts, regulatory filings, operational records,...
item.dc:creator: Manish Sharma

item.title: Prioritize security alerts with runtime context from Dynatrace
item.description: You can now use runtime context from Dynatrace to prioritize GitHub Advanced Security alerts based on deployed artifacts and runtime risk in your Kubernetes environment. When you connect Dynatrace to… The post Prioritize security alerts with runtime context from Dynatrace appeared first on The...
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: Copilot CLI now supports BYOK and local models
item.description: GitHub Copilot CLI now lets you connect your own model provider or run fully local models instead of using GitHub-hosted model routing. This means you can use the models and… The post Copilot CLI now supports BYOK and local models appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: Dependabot alerts are now assignable to AI agents for remediation
item.description: Some dependency vulnerabilities require more than a version bump—they need code changes across your project. You can now assign Dependabot alerts to AI coding agents, including Copilot, Claude, and Codex,… The post Dependabot alerts are now assignable to AI agents for remediation appeared first on...
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: Dependabot version updates now support the Nix ecosystem
item.description: Dependabot now supports Nix flakes. Add nix as a package ecosystem in your dependabot.yml file. Dependabot will then monitor your flake.lock inputs and open pull requests when newer commits are… The post Dependabot version updates now support the Nix ecosystem appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: Take full control of your floating windows in Visual Studio
item.description: Make Visual Studio floating windows work perfectly with PowerToys FancyZones. Flip one option to get independent windows, better snapping, and less friction. The post Take full control of your floating windows in Visual Studio appeared first on Visual Studio Blog.
item.dc:creator: Mads Kristensen

item.title: SOHO router compromise leads to DNS hijacking and adversary-in-the-middle attacks
item.description: Executive summary Forest Blizzard, a threat actor linked to the Russian military, has been compromising insecure home and small-office internet equipment like routers, then modifying their settings in ways that turn them into part of the actor’s malicious infrastructure. The post SOHO router...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft Threat Intelligence

item.title: Code scanning: Batch apply security alert suggestions on pull requests
item.description: GitHub code scanning alerts on pull requests are now easier to address with bulk actions. You can now apply fixes for code scanning alerts in the Files changed tab by… The post Code scanning: Batch apply security alert suggestions on pull requests appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: npm trusted publishing now supports CircleCI
item.description: npm trusted publishing now supports CircleCI as an OIDC provider, joining GitHub Actions and GitLab CI/CD. Maintainers publishing from CircleCI workflows can now eliminate stored credentials entirely and authenticate directly… The post npm trusted publishing now supports CircleCI appeared first on...
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: AI Inference on AKS enabled by Azure Arc: Predictive AI using Triton and ResNet-50
item.description: Run predictive AI at the edge by deploying Triton with the ONNX backend on AKS enabled by Azure Arc for local ResNet 50 GPU inference without cloud dependency.
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: AI Inference on AKS enabled by Azure Arc: Generative AI with Open‑Source LLM Server
item.description: Run generative AI at the edge with Ollama and vLLM on AKS enabled by Azure Arc, enabling local LLM inference on GPU hardware without dependency or data egress.
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: AI Inference on AKS enabled by Azure Arc: Series Introduction and Scope
item.description: Scenario driven series for generative and predictive AI inference on AKS enabled by Azure Arc, across CPUs, GPUs, and NPUs in on-premises and edge environments.
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: AI Inference on AKS enabled by Azure Arc: Bringing AI to the Edge and On‑Premises
item.description: Learn how to bring AI inference closer to your data with AKS enabled by Azure Arc, with practical hybrid and edge scenarios for secure, low-latency workloads.
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Copilot usage metrics now identify active and passive Copilot code review users
item.description: Copilot usage metrics now indicate which users have Copilot code review (CCR) activity, and whether that activity was active or passive. Enterprise and organization admins can see how users engage… The post Copilot usage metrics now identify active and passive Copilot code review users appeared...
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: GitHub Copilot CLI combines model families for a second opinion
item.description: Discover how Rubber Duck provides a different perspective to GitHub Copilot CLI. The post GitHub Copilot CLI combines model families for a second opinion appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Nick McKenna

item.title: Announcing the end of support for Node.js 20.x in the Azure SDK for JavaScript
item.description: After July 9, 2026, the Azure SDK for JavaScript will no longer support Node.js 20.x. Upgrade to an Active Node.js Long Term Support (LTS) version to stay secure and up-to-date. The post Announcing the end of support for Node.js 20.x in the Azure SDK for JavaScript appeared first on Azure SDK Blog.
item.dc:creator: Minh-Anh Phan

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Network Security Perimeter for Azure Service Bus
item.description: Network Security Perimeter (NSP) support for Azure Service Bus is now generally available. Network Security Perimeter creates a logical network boundary around your Service Bus namespaces and other Azure PaaS resources, blocking unauthorized public access
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Inside an AI‑enabled device code phishing campaign
item.description: A new wave of device code phishing shows how threat actors are scaling account compromise using AI and end‑to‑end automation. This campaign goes beyond traditional phishing by generating live authentication codes on demand, enabling higher success rates and sustained post‑compromise access. The...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft Defender Security Research Team

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Rule impact analysis on Azure Network Watcher
item.description: Rule impact analysis on Azure Network Watcher enables you to preview the impact of security admin rules before applying them to your environment. This feature helps you validate rule behavior, identify potential conflicts, and ensure that connectivity req
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Storm-1175 focuses gaze on vulnerable web-facing assets in high-tempo Medusa ransomware operations
item.description: The financially motivated cybercriminal threat actor Storm-1175 operates high-velocity ransomware campaigns that weaponize recently disclosed vulnerabilities to obtain initial access, exfiltrate data, and deploy Medusa ransomware. The post Storm-1175 focuses gaze on vulnerable web-facing assets in...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft Threat Intelligence

item.title: MCP Apps on Azure Functions: Quickstart with TypeScript
item.description: Learn how to build and deploy MCP (Model Context Protocol) apps on Azure Functions using TypeScript. This guide covers MCP tools, resources, local development, and serverless deployment with a practical weather app example. The post MCP Apps on Azure Functions: Quickstart with TypeScript appeared...
item.dc:creator: Swapnil Nagar

item.title: Organization runner controls for Copilot cloud agent
item.description: Each time Copilot cloud agent works on a task, it starts a new development environment powered by GitHub Actions. By default, this runs on a standard GitHub-hosted runner, but teams… The post Organization runner controls for Copilot cloud agent appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: The uphill climb of making diff lines performant
item.description: The path to better performance is often found in simplicity. The post The uphill climb of making diff lines performant appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Luke Ghenco

item.title: Explore union types in C# 15
item.description: C# 15 introduces union types — declare a closed set of case types with implicit conversions and exhaustive pattern matching. Try unions in preview today and see the broader exhaustiveness roadmap. The post Explore union types in C# 15 appeared first on .NET Blog.
item.dc:creator: Bill Wagner

item.title: Aspire Docs in Your Terminal (and Your AI’s Brain)
item.description: Aspire 13.2 ships aspire docs, a CLI for browsing, searching, and reading official aspire.dev docs from your terminal, with the same source of truth available to AI skills and automation. The post Aspire Docs in Your Terminal (and Your AI’s Brain) appeared first on Aspire Blog.
item.dc:creator: David Pine

item.title: Retirement: Python support for Azure App Service and Azure Functions on Windows will be retired on March 31, 2027
item.description: Starting March 31, 2027, Python applications hosted on Azure App Service on Windows and Azure Functions on Windows will no longer run. After this date: Your application configuration and content will remain intact Your apps will stop running
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Retirement: AzureDnsEndpoints for Azure storage accounts will be retired March 2027
item.description: AzureDnsZone endpoints (preview) for Azure Blob storage accounts will enter retirement in March 2027. Customers leveraging AzureDnsZone endpoints (preview) should transition to Standard endpoints for all new and existing blob storage account deployments b
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Threat actor abuse of AI accelerates from tool to cyberattack surface
item.description: Generative AI is upgrading cyberattacks, from 450% higher phishing click‑through rates to industrialized MFA bypass. The post Threat actor abuse of AI accelerates from tool to cyberattack surface appeared first on Microsoft Security Blog.
item.dc:creator: Sherrod DeGrippo

item.title: Cookie-controlled PHP webshells: A stealthy tradecraft in Linux hosting environments
item.description: Cookie-gated PHP webshells use obfuscation, php-fpm execution, and cron-based persistence to evade detection in Linux hosting environments. This post examines how this tradecraft conceals execution behind specially crafted HTTP cookies. The post Cookie-controlled PHP webshells: A stealthy...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft Defender Security Research Team

item.title: Retirement: Service Fabric support for Windows Server 2022 ends on March 31, 2027 - upgrade to Windows Server 2025
item.description: Service Fabric will end support for all clusters running on Windows Server 2022 on March 31, 2027. Customers must upgrade to Windows Server 2025 by this date to remain in a supported state. This change aligns Service Fabric’s Windows OS image support mode
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Mitigating the Axios npm supply chain compromise
item.description: On March 31, 2026, the popular HTTP client Axios experienced a supply chain attack, causing two newly published npm packages for version updates to download from command and control (C2) that Microsoft Threat Intelligence has attributed to the North Korean state actor Sapphire Sleet. Although the...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft Threat Intelligence and Microsoft Defender Security Research Team
